Class VariableKit

java.lang.Object
recoder.kit.VariableKit
public class VariableKit extends Object
this class implements basic functions for type reference handling.

  • Method Summary

    creates a new variable name which does not exist within the given scope.

  • Method Details

    • createValidVariableName

      public static String createValidVariableName(SourceInfo si, ProgramElement context, String guess)
      creates a new variable name which does not exist within the given scope using the given guess as a name base.
      Parameters:
      si - the source information service to be used
      context - the element that defines the scope
      guess - the variable name to be used if possible. If a variable with that name already exists, the method uses suffix numbers.
      Returns:
      a valid variable name within the given scope.

    • createValidVariableName

      public static String createValidVariableName(SourceInfo si, ProgramElement context)
      creates a new variable name which does not exist within the given scope.
      Parameters:
      si - the source information service to be used
      context - the element that defines the scope
      Returns:
      a valid variable name within the given scope.

    • createVariableDeclaration

      @Deprecated public static VariableDeclaration createVariableDeclaration(ServiceConfiguration sc, ProgramElement context, Type t, String guess)
      Deprecated.
      use createNewVariableName instead
      Creates a declaration for a new variable with the given type and a name similar to the given guess.
      Parameters:
      sc - the service configuration to be used
      context - the element that defines the scope
      t - the type of the variable to be declared.
      guess - the variable name to be used if possible. If a variable with that name already exists, the method uses suffix numbers.
      Returns:
      a valid variable name within the given scope.

    • createVariableDeclaration

      public static VariableDeclaration createVariableDeclaration(ServiceConfiguration sc, ProgramElement context, Type t)
      Creates a new local variable declaration with the given type and a new artificial name.
      Parameters:
      sc - the service configuration to be used.
      context - the element that defines the scope.
      t - the type of the variable to be declared.
      Returns:
      a valid variable name within the given scope.

    • createVariableReference

      public static VariableReference createVariableReference(VariableDeclaration decl)
      Create a reference to the first variable in a variable declaration. If there are multiple declarations (as in "int i = 0, j = 0;"), only the first one is used. This is no problem with e.g. ParameterDeclarations.
      Parameters:
      decl - the declaration to create a reference for.
      Returns:
      a new variable reference to the first specification in the given declaration.

    • getNewVariableName

      public static String getNewVariableName(SourceInfo si, Type type, ProgramElement context)
      Query method that finds a name for a new variable with the given type.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service.
      type - the type of the variable to be declared.
      context - the future context of the variable (defines its scope).
      Returns:
      a variable name that is valid in the given context.

    • getNewVariableNames

      public static String[] getNewVariableNames(SourceInfo si, Type[] types, ProgramElement context)
      Query method that finds names for new variables with the given types.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service.
      types - the types of the variables to be declared.
      context - the future context of the variables.
      Returns:
      an array of disjoint variable names that are all valid in the given context.

    • rename

      @Deprecated public static boolean rename(ChangeHistory ch, CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, VariableSpecification var, String newName)
      Deprecated.
      replaced by recoder.kit.transformation.RenameVariable
      Transformation that renames a variable and all known references to it. The new name should not hide another variable.
      Parameters:
      ch - the change history (may be null).
      xr - the cross referencer service.
      var - the variable specification to be renamed; may not be null.
      newName - the new name for the variable; may not be null and must denote a valid identifier name.
      Returns:
      true, if a rename has been necessary, false otherwise.

    • createVariableReference

      public static VariableReference createVariableReference(SourceInfo si, Variable v, ProgramElement context)
      Factory method that creates a minimal qualified reference to the given variable (or field) for the given context. Returns null if the variable is not accessible in the given context, i.e. from a different type, or hidden by another local variable, or hidden by another inherited field.
      Parameters:
      si - the source info service to be used.
      v - the variable to be referred to.
      context - the context to insert the variable reference later on.

    • getReferences

      public static List<VariableReference> getReferences(CrossReferenceSourceInfo xr, Variable v, NonTerminalProgramElement root, boolean scanTree)
      Query that retrieves all references to a given variable that are contained within the given tree. The specified flag defines the strategy to use: either the cross reference information is filtered, or the cross reference information is collected from the tree. The filtering mode is faster if the tree contains more nodes than there are global references to the given variable.
      Parameters:
      xr - the cross referencer to use.
      v - a variable.
      root - the root of an arbitrary syntax tree.
      scanTree - flag indicating the search strategy; if true, local cross reference information is build, otherwise the global cross reference information is filtered.
      Returns:
      the list of references to the given variable in the given tree, can be empty but not null.
      Since:
      0.63

    • isSerialVersionUID

      public static boolean isSerialVersionUID(NameInfo ni, Field f)
      Updating query that checks if the given field is a serialVersionUID constant.
      Parameters:
      ni - the NameInfo service to use.
      f - the field to check.
      Returns:
      true if the given field is a serial version UID of a type, false otherwise.
